title: Betsy Jelinek
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Betsy Jelinek
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

education_entries: Ph D in Philosophy, Duke University
research_interests: Dr. Jelinek received her BA from Wellesley College and her PhD from Duke University. Her research explores the intersection of science, epistemology, and metaphysics in Plato's natural philosophy, and her work has appeared in journals such as Apeiron and History of Philosophy Quarterly.
teaching_interests: Dr. Jelinek's passion for her research fuels her enthusiasm for teaching both philosophy classes and Honors seminars. She has won several awards for her teaching, including the Provost’s Award for Excellence in Teaching.
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal The Retrospective Assignment of Rationality to the Socratic Daimonion . Dialogoi: Ancient Philosophy Today. Volume, 7. Issue, 2. Pages, 141-161.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2024). A Gender Metaphor from Hippocratic Medicine in Plato's Timaeus . Heidelberg, Germany, The Universitätsverlag Winter. Volume, 14. Pages, 71-88.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2023). “Explanation in the Phaedo: An Argument Against the Metaphysical Interpretation of the Clever Αἰτία.” . Routledge. Pages, 162-179.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2022). Evil, Demiurgy, and the Taming of Necessity in Plato’s Timaeus . International Philosophical Quarterly. Pages, 32 pages.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2020). Hippocrates at Phaedrus 270c . Pacific Philosophical Quarterly. Volume, 101. Issue, 3.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2018). Critical Thinking Can Save Your Life . Hackett Publishing Company. Pages, 202-212.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2016). What is Plato’s Epistemic Worry About Phantasia? . Southwest Philosophy Review. Volume, 32. Issue, 2. Pages, 61-67.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2016). Is the Form of the Good a Final Cause for Plato? . History of Philosophy Quarterly. Volume, 33. Issue, 2. Pages, 99-116.
Other (2015). A Commentary on Phantasia as Perception-Based Belief and the Epistemic Worry of Plato . Vanderbilt University.
Other (2015). A Challenge for Vlastos’s Interpretation of Forms in the Phaedo . Harvard University.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2015). A Commentary On "Socrates and his Daimonion: A Paragon of Rationality?" . Southwest Philosophy Review. Volume, 31. Issue, 2.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2015). An Examination of Plato's Chora . Environment, Space, Place. Volume, 7. Issue, 1.
Other Plato's Phaedrus . City College of New York.
Journal Article, Academic Journal Is the Form of the Good a Final Cause for Plato? . History of Philosophy Quarterly.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2014). Evaluating the Goodness of Actions On Plato's Ethical Theory . Philosophical Inquiry: An International Quarterly. Volume, 39. Issue, 4.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2013). Using Group Work in the Philosophy Classroom . Teaching Philosophy. Volume, 36. Issue, 2. Pages, 22.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2011). Pre-Cosmic Necessity in Plato's Timaeus . Apeiron. Volume, 44. Issue, March 2011. Pages, 287-306.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2010). The Philosopher-Ruler: from Theory to Action . Southwest Philosophical Review. Volume, 26. Issue, 1. Pages, 225-232.
awards_honors: Caring for Community Award, Student Leadership Awards. (2022)
Provost's Award for Excellence in Teaching, CNU Provost. (2016)
Class of 2013 Faculty Development Fund Award, Class of 2013. (2014)
Provost Faculty Award for Teaching Writing, Provost and Writing Program Council. (2013)

Presentations: Elizabeth Jelinek, Nickolas Pappas, 18th London Ancient Science Conference 2025, "Keynote Address: Receptacle Dynamics in Plato's Timaeus," London, UK. (2025)
